articles.title: The 1-Minute Market Report, June 7, 2026
articles.author: Erik Conley
articles.summary: S&P 500's 9-week winning streak ended with a sharp sell-off after a blowout May jobs report (172K vs 88K expected). SPY -2.58%, QQQ -4.80%, DIA -1.35%, IWM -3.55%. Risk-off rotation: investors fled high-beta/momentum/growth into value, defensives, and energy. 5 of 12 equity model indicators now yellow, signaling possible market topping phase. Author remains 90% equities, 10% cash with 'Plan B' ready.

articles.title: Weekly Trader's Stock Market Outlook
articles.author: Nathan Peterson, Jim Ferraioli
articles.summary: Largest one-day drop this year. S&P 500 rallied 20.6% from March 30 to Tuesday's ATH without a healthy pullback. Blowout May nonfarm payrolls +172K triggered yield spike: 2-year hit 52-week high (4.164%), 10-year up ~10bps to 4.53%. VIX spiked 28%. Rate hike probability for October FOMC surged to 66.2% from 31.5%. AVGO fell 20% after lukewarm guidance. Alphabet launched $85B equity raise for AI. Meta considering multi-billion equity offering. SOX dropped 9.5% in one day. Near-term SPX technical: moderately bearish. Forecast: 'Higher Volatility' for next week.

articles.title: Stocks fall sharply as strong jobs data fuels rate hike bets
articles.author: Chibuike Oguh et al.
articles.summary: Wall Street plummeted on blowout May jobs report. Dow -1.4%, S&P 500 -2.64%, Nasdaq -4.2%. 2-year Treasury hit 15-month high at 4.147%. Brent crude -2% to $93.09, WTI -2.69% to $90.54. Gold -3.38% to $4,322.85. Bitcoin -3.88% to $61,156 (on track for ~18% weekly decline, largest since FTX). Tech wipeout erased $1.3 trillion in stock market value. Wells Fargo strategist: 'Strong economy adds to inflation risk from the Gulf. Makes it difficult for Fed to even think about rate cuts.'

articles.title: Nasdaq plunges 4%, Dow and S&P 500 sink as AI trade halts on Fed hike bets
articles.summary: Nasdaq -4.1% (worst day since April 2025). S&P 500 snapped 9-week win streak. Jobs report +172K vs ~88K expected. Traders now fully pricing 25bps hike by December. NVDA -6%, market cap slipped below $5T (~$300B wiped). AVGO -4.6% after -13% Thursday. Chip stocks erased $1T in value. Bitcoin below 200-day MA for first time since 2023 at ~$61K. Lululemon tanked >10% after cutting guidance. Marine fuel crisis emerging: 'regional stock-outs by July.'

articles.title: 2026 Mid-Year Outlook: U.S. Stocks and Economy
articles.author: Liz Ann Sonders, Kevin Gordon
articles.summary: Economic growth rebounding but consumers strained by negative real wage growth, weak savings (2.6%), rising energy costs. Inflation sticky: supercore at 3.5% YoY. AI capex adding to inflation as a demand shock alongside Iran war supply shock. S&P 500 earnings growth forecast for FY2026 surged to 25%. But concentration risk extreme: only ~17% of stocks outperformed index. Hyperscaler capex: $800B in 2026 (80% increase), $900B+ in 2027. Capex reaching ~75% of cash flows — reminiscent of late-1990s. Markets vulnerable to disappointment with stretched positioning, thin equity risk premium, rising bond yields.

articles.title: 2026 Midyear Investment Outlook: Constructive, Not Complacent
articles.author: Serena Tang et al.
articles.summary: Overweight equities, underweight core fixed income. S&P 500 year-end 2026 target raised to 8,000 (from 7,800). Mid-2027 forecast: 8,300 (12% gain). Q1 earnings beat by 6% — strongest in 4 years. AI capex estimates revised sharply higher: 2026 $800B, 2027 $1.16T. Rising corporate debt issuance for AI may pressure credit. Dollar likely to weaken near-term on moderating inflation/rate expectations, recovering into 2027. Oil and gas prices expected elevated through year-end even if Hormuz exports begin recovering. Main risk: acceleration in inflation to levels Fed cannot ignore.

articles.title: June 2026 Market Update: Stocks and Inflation Keep Rising
articles.author: Steve Lowe, CFA
articles.summary: May markets gained broadly on strong Q1 earnings (~30% YoY) and AI enthusiasm. But consumer sentiment hit record low (44.8). Savings rate dropped to 2.6%. Real disposable income declining. Core CPI 2.8%, Headline 3.8% (highest since 2024). Core PPI 5.2%, Headline PPI 6.0%. 30-year yield briefly hit ~5.2% (GFC-era levels). Q1 GDP revised down to 1.6%. Fed expected to hold in June but hike expectations rising. Overweight equities, favor large-cap, moderate overweight mid-cap. Caution warranted on Middle East volatility.

articles.title: Economic Outlook - June 2026
articles.summary: Hotter inflation worried markets in May. April PPI surged 1.4% MoM (vs 0.5% expected). Headline CPI 3.8% YoY (highest since May 2023). Core PCE 3.3% (highest since Nov 2023). Bond yields spiked: 10-year added >50bps since Iran War began; 30-year at highest since pre-GFC 2007. 2-year above 4% since mid-May. Fed minutes revealed heightened inflation concerns. Fed funds futures pricing one rate increase by December. Gasoline $4.49/gal (up 42% YoY). Personal income flat vs +0.4% expected. Durable goods orders +7.9% (strong, helped by AI capex). GDP Q1 revised to 1.6%.

articles.title: Oil prices rise as Iran reviews US proposal to halt war
articles.summary: Brent rose to $96.00 (highest since May 26), WTI to $93.76. Iran examining US proposal but communications paused. Trump claims deal within a week. Ritterbusch: 'significant reopening of Strait doesn't appear much closer than months ago.' IEA warns global oil inventories could hit critical levels ahead of peak summer demand. ~20% of global oil/LNG flows disrupted. OPEC+ set for fourth oil quota hike since Hormuz closure. Conflicting signals from White House, Iran, Israel keeping markets on edge.

articles.title: Stock market 'euphoria' masks looming Iran war recession risk
articles.author: CNBC
articles.summary: Investors underplaying Iran war economic impact. S&P 500 hit ATH of 7,230 on May 1 despite >50% oil surge since Feb 28. Amrita Sen (Energy Aspect): 'Sleepwalking into potentially a pretty big recession. Extremely misplaced euphoria.' Oil at Brent $111, WTI $104. OPEC production increase 'largely symbolic.' Strait of Hormuz disruption requires demand destruction to 2013 levels. Morgan Stanley's Eisenschidt: 'nearing a day of reckoning.' ECB rate hike likely if no resolution in 1-2 weeks. New oil floor: $80-90 long-term. Collateral damage: LNG, chemicals, fertilizers, food prices.

articles.title: SpaceX targets $135 IPO price at valuation of $1.77 trillion
articles.summary: SpaceX sets fixed IPO price of $135/share (not a range). 555.6M shares offered, raising $75B (+$11.2B underwriter option). Valuation: $1.77T (7th largest US company, surpassing Tesla). Nasdaq debut June 12, ticker SPCX. Musk retains 82%+ voting control. Lead underwriter: Goldman Sachs + Morgan Stanley, BofA, Citi, JPM. Largest IPO ever (>3x Alibaba). 5% shares reserved for employees. Competitive context: Anthropic filed confidentially June 1, OpenAI preparing filing. Speculation about future SpaceX-Tesla combination.

articles.title: 2026 Credit Outlook: From Scarcity to Selection
articles.author: John Cortese et al.
articles.summary: Credit markets undergoing regime shift: from scarcity to higher-supply buyer's market. Hyperscaler investment tripled since 2023. Cumulative AI spending >$2.7T (2025-2029). Internal cash flows insufficient; external debt financing now primary driver across IG, private credit, project finance, CRE, ABS. AI issuance creating concentration and correlation risk — apparent diversification across sectors reflects single macro bet on AI. K-shaped economy driving dispersion, not distress. Opportunity areas: European private credit, sports financing.

articles.title: Stock market next week: Outlook for June 1-5, 2026
articles.summary: Wall Street entered June at all-time highs on Iran peace hopes and AI growth. Nasdaq up >8% since April. Q1 S&P 500 earnings blended growth 28%+ (IT sector 54%+). Iran war resolution expected within 2-3 weeks — political pressure in midterm election year. Risk factors: consumer health deteriorating (savings, wage growth), June historically worst month in midterm election years. Key quote: 'The real bet is we're coming to resolution on Iran, slowly but surely, in the next two, three weeks.'

articles.title: BMO Commodities Outlook for June 2026
articles.summary: Commodities outlook for June 2026 from BMO Capital Markets. World Bank data: non-energy index rose 2.5%, metals +3.7%, precious metals -1.7%. Deutsche Bank forecasts 2026 average oil at $61.50/b but this predates current war-driven levels. Trading Economics consensus shows broad commodity price elevation driven by geopolitical supply disruptions. Next World Bank update: July 2, 2026.

articles.title: Weekly Economic Calendar: June 8-14, 2026
articles.summary: KEY EVENT: US CPI on Wednesday June 10. Recent headline CPI 3.8% YoY, core 2.8%. Monday June 8: no major releases (51 earnings though). Tuesday: China trade balance. Wednesday: China CPI + US CPI + BoC rate decision (current 2.25%). Thursday: ECB rate decision (main refinancing 2.15%) + US PPI. Friday: German HICP final + U. Michigan sentiment prelim. Fed's next FOMC meeting June 16-17 (Kevin Warsh's first as chair). BoC likely to pause; ECB facing hawkish pressure from energy inflation but recession risks.
